capture_policy.purpose: Replayable maintenance evidence captured from real repository history and commands executed in a clean worktree.
capture_policy.prompt_rule: Historical prompts are labeled commit_derived_reconstruction unless a verbatim maintainer request is available.
capture_policy.adoption_rule: Adoption means a reachable main-history commit, release tag, or passing repository gate; it is not presented as a fabricated human conversation.

records.task: Review the v3.1 reviewer narrative, contributor templates, and Codex maintainer guidance before integration.

input.prompt: Review the v3.1 repository narrative and maintainer-facing documents for accuracy, traceability, and sustainable contribution workflow coverage.

skills_used.name: code-review-workflow
skills_used.role: Assess the change set and its reviewer-facing contract.

skills_used.name: requirements-clarifier
skills_used.role: Check that public claims map to verifiable repository evidence.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the README, AGENTS guidance, and case study as the public review surface.
Inspected the commit scope to distinguish documentation, templates, and validation changes.
Ran the ecosystem gate that checks the reviewer-facing assets and required markers.
Returned an acceptance decision tied to the passing ecosystem gate and adopted commit.

files_read.purpose: Verify the 60-second public reviewer path.
Verify Codex review, release, and security behavior.
Verify that the application narrative cites evidence instead of promises.

commands.interpretation: The public review and governance assets passed their repository contract.

final_output.summary: The reviewer narrative was accepted because its public claims, maintainer rules, and contribution surfaces were all covered by an executable ecosystem check.

human_conclusion.summary: Repository adoption is evidenced by this reviewed change being reachable from main; no separate human-chat transcript is claimed.

score.rationale: The record keeps the task, behavior, immutable source files, replayed gate, output excerpt, and adoption evidence together.

records.task: Review the v3.0 public release surface for changelog, release-note, and artifact consistency before publishing.

input.prompt: Review the v3.0 release-facing change set and confirm that public documentation, release notes, and retained artifacts tell the same story.

skills_used.name: code-review-workflow
skills_used.role: Review release-facing changes for consistency and risk.

skills_used.name: skill-pipeline-orchestrator
skills_used.role: Connect the review decision to release artifact verification.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the public README, changelog, and retained v3.0 release notes.
Inspected the release publication commit and its tag relationship.
Ran the release-archive gate that verifies artifacts and their trust sidecars.
Returned an acceptance decision linked to the tagged release and archive evidence.

files_read.purpose: Confirm the public release framing.
Confirm released behavior is recorded for maintainers.
Confirm release notes point to real artifacts and verification.

commands.recorded_output_excerpt:
[OK] v3.0.0: 14 skills, artifact and sidecars consistent
[OK] Release archive is internally consistent
replay_markers: [OK] v3.0.0: 14 skills, artifact and sidecars consistent
commands.interpretation: The retained v3.0 archive has matching artifacts, manifests, and provenance sidecars.

final_output.summary: The v3.0 public release surface was accepted because the release archive check connects documentation claims to a consistent stored artifact set.

human_conclusion.summary: The v3.0.0 tag and reachable publication commit are the repository evidence that this reviewed release decision was adopted.

score.rationale: This review ties release-facing source files to an immutable commit and a replayed archive gate.

records.task: Review the CI portability fix that changed release archive checks and full-release verification behavior.

input.prompt: Review the CI portability fix for cross-platform artifact verification and make sure the release gate remains readable and safe to merge.

skills_used.name: code-review-workflow
skills_used.role: Review correctness and regression risk in CI-facing code.

skills_used.name: test-design-workflow
skills_used.role: Check that portability claims have an executable verification path.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the archive verifier, release verifier, and generated provenance sidecar.
Inspected the portability commit scope before evaluating merge readiness.
Ran Git whitespace validation for the reviewed change-set contract.
Returned a merge-ready decision with the cleanliness gate as evidence.

files_read.purpose: Inspect archive consistency checks changed by the portability fix.
Inspect the one-command verification contract.
Inspect the release provenance affected by portability normalization.

commands.interpretation: Git reported no whitespace errors, preserving the reviewed diff cleanliness contract.

final_output.summary: The portability change-set was accepted after its release verification paths were read and the diff cleanliness gate produced no errors.

human_conclusion.summary: The reachable main-history commit is the adoption evidence for this PR-style review; no fabricated remote pull request number is used.

score.rationale: The change-set review is reproducible from immutable files, a fixed command, and a main-history adoption record.

records.task: Triage a CI failure caused by generated verification outputs that depended on the local calendar date.

input.prompt: Investigate why generated reports pass locally but fail in CI after a date boundary, then identify the smallest deterministic fix.

skills_used.name: systematic-debugging
skills_used.role: Reproduce and isolate the non-deterministic report behavior.

skills_used.name: observability-workflow
skills_used.role: Use generated report checks as the observable regression signal.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the report generators and release builder that emitted calendar-sensitive metadata.
Inspected the follow-up fix that removed volatile verification output fields.
Ran the platform report freshness check after the deterministic-output repair.
Reported the root cause, deterministic remediation, and passing verification marker.

files_read.purpose: Inspect generated platform report metadata.
Inspect generated example dataset metadata.
Inspect release-side date normalization.

commands.interpretation: The deterministic report check now succeeds without rewriting committed outputs.

final_output.summary: The incident was resolved by removing volatile generated fields from checked-in outputs while keeping release metadata intentionally time-based.

human_conclusion.summary: The deterministic-output repair is adopted on main and is tied to the public CI recovery commit.

score.rationale: This triage record preserves the incident task, source fix, replayable check, and adopted remediation.

records.task: Triage a one-command CI verification failure caused by workspace cleanliness and release sidecar drift.

input.prompt: Find why the one-command release verification does not leave CI clean, then verify the release sidecars still agree after the fix.

skills_used.name: systematic-debugging
skills_used.role: Trace the failing verification path to its workspace-state cause.

skills_used.name: git-workflow-for-agents
skills_used.role: Validate the clean-tree and commit-sidecar contract.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the CI workflow, full verification script, and release manifest involved in the failure.
Inspected the cleanliness fix and the exact release-sidecar changes it introduced.
Ran the archive consistency gate after workspace and sidecar normalization.
Reported that the follow-up commit makes the one-command path compatible with a clean CI checkout.

files_read.purpose: Inspect CI execution order and clean-tree expectations.
Inspect the one-command release verification behavior.
Inspect the repaired release manifest sidecar.

commands.interpretation: The retained release artifacts remain internally consistent after the CI cleanliness repair.

final_output.summary: The issue triage found a clean-workspace mismatch and verified that the resulting sidecar updates preserve the release archive contract.

human_conclusion.summary: The main-history cleanup commit is the adoption evidence for this CI triage and its artifact-sidecar repair.

score.rationale: The record makes the CI incident, examined sources, command evidence, and adopted resolution independently inspectable.

records.task: Triage platform-report hash portability so artifacts produced on different systems verify consistently in CI.

input.prompt: Investigate platform-report hash differences across environments and confirm that the generated index and release sidecars stay reproducible.

skills_used.name: systematic-debugging
skills_used.role: Isolate the platform-dependent hash behavior.

skills_used.name: cross-model-verification
skills_used.role: Require a second reproducibility signal instead of accepting a local-only result.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the platform report generator, marketplace index, and affected release manifest.
Inspected the hash-portability patch and associated regenerated sidecars.
Ran the platform report currentness check as the replayable portability signal.
Reported the portability root cause and the passing deterministic verification result.

files_read.purpose: Inspect the platform-specific hashing behavior.
Inspect the generated index affected by hash normalization.
Inspect the regenerated release manifest.

commands.interpretation: The current report check confirms that portability normalization no longer rewrites committed output.

final_output.summary: The portability triage resolved platform-dependent hash drift and retained a check that exposes future report regressions before merge.

human_conclusion.summary: The reachable main-history portability commit is the adoption evidence for the report hash remediation.

score.rationale: The record connects a specific reproducibility incident to immutable implementation artifacts and a replayed report gate.

records.task: Audit the v3.0 release artifact, checksum, manifest, and provenance before treating the tag as trustworthy.

input.prompt: Validate the v3.0 artifact set before release acceptance, including manifest, checksum, provenance, and retained package consistency.

skills_used.name: skill-pipeline-orchestrator
skills_used.role: Coordinate release verification stages and evidence handoff.

skills_used.name: agent-security-guard
skills_used.role: Check trust sidecars and release integrity assumptions.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the v3.0 manifest, checksum, and provenance sidecars retained with the release.
Inspected the tagged publication commit before accepting the release evidence.
Ran the release archive consistency gate against every retained release.
Returned an artifact-audit result that identifies the v3.0 trust sidecars as consistent.

files_read.purpose: Verify artifact identity and manifest structure.
Verify the stored artifact checksum.
Verify retained build and source provenance.

commands.recorded_output_excerpt:
[OK] v3.0.0: 14 skills, artifact and sidecars consistent
[OK] Release archive is internally consistent
replay_markers: [OK] v3.0.0: 14 skills, artifact and sidecars consistent
commands.interpretation: The archive verifier confirms the artifact and trust sidecars remain mutually consistent.

final_output.summary: The v3.0 release audit verified that the retained artifact, checksum, manifest, and provenance sidecars form a consistent release record.

human_conclusion.summary: The v3.0.0 tag and publication commit show that the verified artifact set was accepted as the project release baseline.

score.rationale: The release record makes the concrete artifact sidecars and archive validation available for independent replay.

records.task: Check the marketplace release inventory before an installation, update, or rollback workflow is offered to maintainers.

input.prompt: Confirm that the published marketplace inventory is complete and aligns with the release verifier before exercising install and rollback flows.

skills_used.name: skill-pipeline-orchestrator
skills_used.role: Coordinate the marketplace and release verification stages.

skills_used.name: agent-config-reference
skills_used.role: Check the Codex marketplace installation target contract.

agent_behavior.detail: Read marketplace operations, full release verification, and the v3.0 manifest contract.
Inspected the current main commit that made generated verification output deterministic.
Listed the live marketplace inventory as a release gate input.
Reported that all fourteen registered skills are visible to the marketplace release surface.

files_read.purpose: Inspect supported marketplace list, install, update, and rollback actions.
Inspect the end-to-end release and rollback verification sequence.
Inspect the published release inventory baseline.

commands.interpretation: The marketplace lists the published release and its active maintenance workflow inventory.

final_output.summary: The marketplace inventory gate verified that the published release exposes the expected active skills before downstream install and rollback operations.

human_conclusion.summary: The reachable main commit and passing inventory gate are the repository evidence for accepting this release workflow result.

score.rationale: The record connects marketplace inventory output to the scripts and manifest that define the release workflow.

records.task: Run the public-upload readiness gate before presenting the project as a maintainable open-source release.

input.prompt: Check public repository readiness for the release surface, including governance files, CI entry points, and reviewer documentation.

skills_used.name: skill-pipeline-orchestrator
skills_used.role: Coordinate publication-gate evidence across repository surfaces.

skills_used.name: git-workflow-for-agents
skills_used.role: Check public upload and clean repository conventions.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the publication readiness checker, CI workflow, and public README entry point.
Inspected the adopted main state used for public repository verification.
Ran the publication readiness command and retained its success marker.
Reported that the public repository surface meets the configured publication contract.

files_read.purpose: Inspect required public files and forbidden upload material checks.
Inspect the public CI verification entry point.
Inspect the public quickstart and evidence links.

commands.recorded_output_excerpt: [OK] Publication-ready check passed across 136 public files
commands.interpretation: The repository met its configured public-upload and governance-file requirements.

final_output.summary: The public readiness gate passed, demonstrating that the release is supported by governance, CI, and reviewer-facing documentation rather than code alone.

human_conclusion.summary: The current main history and passing publication gate are the repository evidence for accepting this release readiness result.

score.rationale: This release gate preserves the exact public files inspected and an independently replayable publication command.

records.task: Audit secret-redaction coverage and scan the repository for material that should never be committed or forwarded.

input.prompt: Run the repository security scan and review its redaction regression cases to confirm that example data cannot silently normalize unsafe secret handling.

skills_used.name: agent-security-guard
skills_used.role: Gate secret exposure and risky repository content.

skills_used.name: cross-model-verification
skills_used.role: Require explicit evidence for security conclusions.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the security scanner, redaction regression cases, and security skill guidance.
Inspected the main commit used as the security audit source snapshot.
Ran the repository security scan and captured its passing output marker.
Reported that the scanner and redaction cases passed without a new security finding.

files_read.purpose: Inspect the repository secret and unsafe-content scanning implementation.
Inspect regression cases for outbound-data redaction behavior.
Inspect the agent-facing safety workflow used for the audit.

commands.recorded_output_excerpt: [OK] Security scan passed across 117 text files
commands.interpretation: The security scanner found no prohibited secrets or unsafe content in the audited source tree.

final_output.summary: The redaction and secret-exposure audit passed with concrete scanner output and immutable regression-case provenance.

human_conclusion.summary: The passing security gate and reachable main snapshot are the evidence for accepting this security audit conclusion.

score.rationale: The audit record keeps security-specific source files, command evidence, and an explicit no-finding conclusion together.

records.task: Audit the dangerous-command policy and its agent-facing guard before relying on automated maintenance actions.

input.prompt: Review the dangerous-command policy, its regression cases, and the agent safety guard; verify the repository gate still rejects unsafe patterns.

skills_used.name: agent-security-guard
skills_used.role: Interpret dangerous-command and data-handling constraints.

skills_used.name: code-review-workflow
skills_used.role: Review policy behavior and enforcement coverage.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the dangerous-command policy, scanner implementation, and agent safety skill.
Inspected the stable main snapshot used for policy verification.
Ran the repository security scan that enforces policy and redaction checks.
Reported that the dangerous-command policy remained enforced by a passing security gate.

files_read.purpose: Inspect the explicit allowlist and denylist policy contract.
Inspect policy enforcement and unsafe-content scan behavior.
Inspect the agent workflow that consumes the safety policy.

commands.recorded_output_excerpt: [OK] Security scan passed across 117 text files
commands.interpretation: The policy enforcement gate completed successfully across the audited repository text files.

final_output.summary: The dangerous-command policy audit verified that the published policy and agent guard remain connected to an executable security gate.

human_conclusion.summary: The main-history source snapshot and passing security gate are the evidence for accepting this policy audit result.

records.task: Audit skill dependency quality and review-bot policy coverage before accepting a broad maintainer workflow change.

input.prompt: Run a code-quality audit of skill dependencies and review automation, then flag isolated skills, unsafe cycles, or missing workflow stages.

skills_used.name: code-review-workflow
skills_used.role: Assess code-quality and graph-level maintenance risks.

skills_used.name: observability-workflow
skills_used.role: Use graph and bot outputs as maintained quality signals.

agent_behavior.detail: Read the skill graph checker, review bot workflow, and code review skill guidance.
Inspected the main snapshot that defines the audited skill dependency graph.
Ran the skill graph quality check and captured its coverage and edge-count output.
Reported that the graph has full registered-skill coverage with no isolated workflow nodes.

files_read.purpose: Inspect dependency graph quality and stage coverage checks.
Inspect the automated code-quality review entry point.
Inspect the maintainer-facing code review procedure.

commands.recorded_output_excerpt: [OK] Skill graph covers 14 skills, 39 edges, and 15 stages
commands.interpretation: The quality graph covers every registered skill and exposes dependency or stage regressions as CI failures.

final_output.summary: The code-quality audit found complete skill graph coverage and preserved a replayable gate for future isolated-node or stage-coverage regressions.

human_conclusion.summary: The reachable main snapshot and passing graph gate are the repository evidence for accepting this code-quality audit conclusion.

score.rationale: The audit provides concrete graph-check code, workflow automation, skill guidance, and a replayed quality result.
